steps.title: Identify what would undermine the hypothesis
steps.content:
Foster's hypothesis depends on an observation: large, well-established otter populations at Vancouver Island create healthier eelgrass meadows with greater genetic diversity.

A finding that would undermine this would show that large, established populations don't consistently produce healthier meadows.

steps.title: Apply the test to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C shows that large, well-established otter populations correlate with unhealthy meadows, contradicting Foster's predicted outcome.

The other choices involve different otter conditions (small or recent in A; absent eelgrass in B) or unrelated species (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
